inline_comments.message:
Here can null pointer occur if status is null. You have two options:

1. while (!timedOut && !CANCELLED.equals(status)) {...

here we enter the while loop if status is null

2. while (!timedOut && status!=null && !status.equals(CANCELLED) { ...

here we do not enter the while loop if status is null

Please analyse the code and decide. I think that the author of the original code assumes that status can be null in the loop, so I would choose first option.

in this case, just make it shorter:
if (CANCELLED.equals(status)) {...
